title: Pak bombs Kabul homes, Kandahar airport in renewed strikes; women, children killed - key details
content: Pakistan launched airstrikes on Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, early Friday, resulting in four deaths and 15 injuries. This latest escalation stems from Pakistan's accusations that the Afghan Taliban government harbors the Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP), which has increased attacks from Afghan territory.

title: From 20 dishes to 15: LPG crunch trims Bengaluru wedding menus
content: Bengaluru's traditional wedding feasts are being impacted by an LPG shortage, forcing caterers to reduce menu items and alter recipes.  This crisis is affecting the preparation of traditional dishes and the ability to maintain both quantity and quality, though wedding bookings remain steady. Caterers hope for a swift stabilization of the LPG supply.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 goes below 23,400; BSE Sensex tumbles 900 points
content: Indian stock markets tumbled Friday, with Nifty50 and BSE Sensex opening sharply lower. Rising oil prices and global trade disruptions fueled investor caution. Experts highlight ongoing West Asian conflict uncertainty and sustained foreign institutional selling as key pressures. Pharmaceuticals emerge as a resilient sector amid the broader market weakness.

title: Google renames London HQ; DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis shares story behind it
content: Google's London headquarters is now Platform 37. The name honours its location and a key AI moment. A new public space, The AI Exchange, will open soon. It will offer exhibitions and educational programmes about artificial intelligence. Teams will move into the building later this year. This marks a significant step for AI development and public engagement.

title: Murder masked as suicide? Woman burns husband alive in TN; CCTV footage exposes truth
content: A shocking murder disguised as suicide has emerged in Kallakurichi. CCTV footage revealed a wife setting her husband ablaze after he discovered her alleged affair. The victim, Antony Robin, suffered fatal burns. Initially, the wife claimed he self-immolated due to intoxication, but the video evidence exposed her deliberate act. Police are investigating a domestic dispute as the motive.

title: Allahabad high court orders 24/7 security for UP man ‘stopped from offering namaz at home’
content: The Allahabad High Court has mandated 24/7 security for a Bareilly man fearing demolition of his private property after being prevented from offering prayers. The court stated any harm to him or his property would be presumed state-instigated, subject to rebuttal. This follows a previous ruling affirming the right to pray on private land without permission.

title: 'No life insurance': Netanyahu's stark warning to Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei & a 'puppet' dig
content: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu issued a stark warning to Iran's new Supreme Leader, calling him a "puppet" and stating there was "no life insurance" for regime leaders. He asserted that the joint US-Israeli military campaign is progressing faster than expected, significantly weakening Iran and creating conditions for potential regime change.

title: Apple to celebrate 50th anniversary on April 1: Read CEO Tim Cook's 'Thank You' letter
content: Apple celebrates its 50th anniversary, marking a remarkable journey from a garage startup to a global tech giant. CEO Tim Cook expressed gratitude to employees, developers, and customers for transforming technology and making it personal. The company's evolution, from early computers to modern services, is a testament to its enduring belief in innovation and empowering individuals to 'think different'.

title: ‘Acknowledging the obvious’: Russia reacts to US waiver on oil amid Iran war
content: Russia asserted that global energy markets cannot be stable without its oil, following a US temporary allowance for Russian crude sales already in transit. This move occurred as oil prices surged amid escalating conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the US, with Iran warning of retaliatory attacks on regional energy infrastructure.

title: After T20 World Cup heroics, can Sanju and Ishan break into India’s ODI plans?
content: As Team India gears up for the 2027 ODI World Cup, questions arise about incorporating T20 stars like Sanju Samson and Ishan Kishan into the ODI setup. Despite recent ODI series losses, their T20 heroics and potential ODI contributions are being debated, with selectors weighing form against established openers and other talented keepers.

title: Two Kukis found dead after MHA intervenes to free Naga 'hostages'
content: In a tragic turn of events in Ukhrul district, Manipur, the discovery of two Kuki men's bodies has sparked renewed ethnic unrest, just days after 21 Naga travellers were released thanks to intervention from the Union Home Ministry. As tensions flared, protesters quickly took to the streets, blocking a major highway.

title: Dell backs Sam Altman on Pentagon deal: Firms can’t control tech use by govt
content: Dell Technologies CEO Michael Dell echoed OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's views on the Pentagon's cancellation of Anthropic's deal. Dell stated companies cannot dictate government technology use, a stance aligning with Altman's belief that Anthropic's demands complicated its Pentagon engagement. This comes as Anthropic sues the Pentagon after being labeled a supply-chain risk.

title: ‘For their own life and safety’: Trump questions Iran’s participation at FIFA World Cup
content: President Trump expressed that it may not be "appropriate" for Iran's national football team to attend the FIFA World Cup due to safety concerns, despite previously assuring FIFA's president of their welcome. The White House has not clarified the specific safety threats Trump alluded to.

title: Bihar: Class X student gang-raped and murdered; was thrown into well
content: A Class X student was allegedly gang-raped and murdered by five individuals in Saran district. The victim was reportedly dragged to a bathroom, assaulted, and then thrown into a well. Following the incident, irate villagers protested until assurances were given, and one suspect was taken into custody.

title: LPG crisis fails to halt Golden Temple’s langar amid Middle East conflict
content: Despite the West Asia conflict impacting LPG supplies, the Golden Temple's 'Guru Ka Langar' continues to serve nearly one lakh devotees daily. The shrine has a reserve of 500-1000 quintals of firewood as a contingency plan, ensuring uninterrupted service even if gas deliveries are delayed.

title: US KC-135 Stratotanker crashes in Iraq: Watch midair refuelling in action, how the process works
content: A US KC-135 refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq with at least five personnel aboard, while a second aircraft landed safely. This incident, not attributed to hostile fire, marks the fourth US aircraft loss since late February. The ongoing conflict has resulted in US troop casualties and injuries, alongside a significant death toll in Iran.

title: Etihad, flydubai, Emirates and Air Arabia run limited flights on March 13
content: Air travel in the UAE faces ongoing disruptions due to regional tensions, with Dubai's airports operating on adjusted schedules. Major airlines like Emirates and Etihad are managing limited flights, prioritizing affected passengers and offering flexible rebooking. Authorities urge travelers to confirm flights before heading to the airport and warn of online scams targeting those impacted.

title: Sacked vendor storms Hindustan Petroleum plant, kills manager & deputy manager
content: Tragedy struck HPCL's Budaun plant as a disgruntled former vendor, Ajay Singh, fatally shot two senior officials, plant manager Sudhir Gupta and deputy manager Harshit Mishra. Singh, allegedly sacked two months prior and facing a prior case, opened fire at close range before fleeing. A manhunt is underway, with security heightened.

title: ‘Not going to close’: Iran’s UN envoy contradicts Khamenei over Strait of Hormuz remarks
content: Iran's UN ambassador stated Tehran will not close the Strait of Hormuz, despite recent remarks from the supreme leader suggesting its use as leverage.  While asserting the right to ensure regional security, the ambassador emphasized Iran's commitment to freedom of navigation under international law, attributing current tensions to US actions.

title: Mustafizur lands Hundred deal as two Pakistan players also picked in auction
content: The Hundred auction saw Mustafizur Rahman snapped up by Birmingham Phoenix for £100,000.  Two Pakistan spinners, Abrar Ahmed (£190,000 to Sunrisers Leeds) and Usman Tariq (£140,000 to Birmingham Phoenix), also found buyers.  James Coles secured the highest bid of £390,000. The tournament runs from July 21 to August 16.

title: 'They were going to take over Middle East': Trump says Iran had 1,200 missiles pointed at 4 nations
content: US President Donald Trump asserted Iran has amassed approximately 1,200 missiles targeting Middle Eastern nations, amidst escalating tensions following threats from Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei. Trump suggested Khamenei, despite injury speculation, is likely alive and urged vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz, stating Iran lacks a significant navy.

title: IMD issues fresh heatwave alert for Mumbai on Friday and Saturday
content: Mumbai braces for another heatwave as the India Meteorological Department issued a warning for Friday and Saturday, following three days of intense heat earlier this month. While Thursday offered a brief respite, temperatures are expected to rise again across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Coastal-interior transition zones are particularly affected by high 'real feel' temperatures.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: NCC, and HFCL are the top stocks that Bajaj Broking Research recommends buying on March 13, 2026. Here is a detailed outlook on NCC, and HFCL along with a view on Nifty and Bank Nifty.

title: Oil price today: Crude crosses $100 per barrel again amid Middle East tensions
content: Middle East tensions are sending global oil prices soaring past $100 a barrel, with Brent crude trading at $100.66.  Attacks on Iran have triggered significant supply concerns, potentially leading to the "largest supply disruption" ever.  Despite economic uncertainty, the US prioritizes confronting Iran over oil prices, while global efforts to mitigate the impact are underway.

title: Who was behind the US synagogue attack? DHS names Lebanon-born Ayman Mohamad Ghazali
content: A man, identified as a naturalized US citizen born in Lebanon, was killed by security after ramming his vehicle into a synagogue near Detroit. While staff and children were present, none were harmed. The FBI is investigating the incident as a targeted act against the Jewish community, though the motive remains unclear.

title: Mother mixes pesticide in drink to threaten husband; 5-year-old drinks it, dies
content: A five-year-old girl in Eluru district, Andhra Pradesh, tragically died after accidentally drinking a soft drink laced with pesticide. Her mother had mixed the poison in the drink to threaten her husband during an argument, but the child unknowingly consumed it. The girl was rushed to the hospital but succumbed to the poisoning.

title: ‘South Africa are the stupidest team’: Former England captain’s explosive remark
content: Michael Vaughan has criticized South Africa's tactical decision to defeat the West Indies in the T20 World Cup, suggesting it inadvertently aided India's progression. Vaughan argued that eliminating strong teams early is crucial for World Cup success. South Africa's win allowed India to advance, ultimately leading to South Africa's semi-final exit against New Zealand.

title: Chennai shocker: Man urinates on woman from auto, mob beats him to death
content: A load van driver was beaten to death in Chennai after urinating on a woman from an auto. The victim's family has refused to accept the body, demanding the arrest of the attackers. Police are reviewing surveillance footage to identify the culprits.

title: Denver Nuggets vs Spurs injury report: Who’s out, questionable
content: The Denver Nuggets face the San Antonio Spurs on March 12, 2026. The Spurs, holding a strong 48-17 record and second place in the Western Conference, are favored by 4.5 points. Both teams are managing injuries, with key players like Victor Wembanyama and Aaron Gordon listed as questionable, potentially impacting rotations for this anticipated matchup.

title: Taylor Swift’s $2B milestone hints at future plans with Travis Kelce
content: Travis Kelce has confirmed he will return to the Kansas City Chiefs for another NFL season, signing a one year deal reportedly worth up to $15 million. The veteran tight end says his decision was driven by his continued love for football. Kelce also credited Taylor Swift’s passion for her craft as inspiration. With the couple expected to marry before training camp, both stars remain committed to thriving in their respective careers rather than slowing down.

title: Fact check: Is Travis Hunter paying $150K monthly to wife Leanna Lenee?
content: Travis Hunter, the Jacksonville Jaguars star player, and his wife, Leanna Lenee, have been married for less than a year and have already found themselves involved in a scandalous debate after rumors of the star player being cheated on by his newly wed wife sparked chaos. Amid this, a clip of Travis Hunter allegedly saying he has to pay her a massive amount monthly to her has sparked chaos.

title: 'I am in love with her': Man nabbed for stealing woman's underwear, tattoos her name
content: A bizarre theft unfolded in Ujjain as a 25-year-old man was apprehended for stealing a woman's innerwear from her clothesline. CCTV footage captured the peculiar act, leading to his identification and arrest. The accused confessed to a one-sided love for the victim, even sporting her name tattooed on his chest.

title: 'He doesn’t even look at me': Ex-BCCI selector opens up on Gambhir fallout after India snub
content: Former chief selector Sandeep Patil revealed Gautam Gambhir hasn't spoken to him since being dropped from the Indian team in 2013. Despite their past closeness and numerous shared events, Gambhir maintains his distance, a decision Patil understands given his own experience of being dropped. Patil emphasized that personal relationships don't influence tough selection calls.

title: 'Targeted act of violence against Jewish community': Shooting at US synagogue; suspect dead
content: Police rushed to Temple Israel near Detroit following reports of an active shooter, with smoke billowing from the synagogue's roof. A truck reportedly crashed into the building, prompting a massive law enforcement response. Jewish institutions across the region were advised to enter a precautionary lockdown as authorities investigate the unfolding security incident.

title: Mandhira Kapur Smith files reply to Priya Sachdev’s defamation suit
content: The courtroom drama surrounding Sunjay Kapur's estate escalates, with his sister Mandhira Kapur Smith insisting that widow Priya Sachdev Kapur produce critical documents in a defamation case. Scheduled for a hearing on March 30, 2026, the court's intervention has sparked debates over the documents' relevance, as Priya's legal counsel contends they are public and should remain unissued. Read on to know more in detail.

title: SGA breaks Wilt's record: The 20 wildest facts of Thunder star's 127 games of dominance
content: With Gilgeous-Alexander holding the record for most consecutive 20-point games, Kram breaks down the stats.

title: SGA eclipses Wilt for NBA's longest 20-point streak
content: Thunder star Shai Gilgeous-Alexander made history Thursday night against the Celtics, breaking Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record with his 127th consecutive regular-season game with at least 20 points.

title: Murray joins Vikings, 'elevating' their QB room
content: A day after the Cardinals officially released him, Kyler Murray signed a one-year contract with the Vikings and will join former first-round pick J.J. McCarthy in the QB room in Minnesota.

title: Big 12 to swap LED glass court for hardwood
content: The Big 12 will transition back to a traditional hardwood court for the remainder of its conference tournament at T-Mobile Center after the innovative LED glass court it was using led to some slipping and grip issues for players.

title: State charges Falcons' Pearce with 3 felonies
content: Falcons defender James Pearce Jr. has been charged with three felonies and a misdemeanor in relation to an incident involving WNBA player Rickea Jackson.

title: Spoelstra sorry to 'absolutely no one' for Bam's 83
content: Heat coach Erik Spoelstra brushed aside critics of Bam Adebayo's 83-point game, saying, "You have to go for it, and that was just thrilling."

title: Leafs star Matthews (knee) injured in 'dirty play'
content: Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews left Thursday night against Anaheim after a knee-on-knee hit from Ducks defenseman Radko Gudas.

title: Sources: K-State set to hire Belmont's Alexander
content: Casey Alexander, a perennial 20-game winner at Belmont, is expected to be named the new coach at Kansas State, sources told ESPN's Jeff Borzello.

title: Pearl after SEC exit: Auburn 'deserves' NCAA bid
content: Auburn coach Steven Pearl lobbied for his team's NCAA tournament chances after blowing a double-digit second-half lead to Tennessee in the SEC tournament quarterfinals Thursday.

title: What Kyler Murray signing means for Vikings, J.J. McCarthy -- and Justin Jefferson's fantasy value
content: Will Murray resurrect his career and boost the Vikings' offense after being released by the Cardinals?

title: Making sense of Crosby trade, Ravens' ensuing Hendrickson signing
content: Maxx Crosby was traded to the Ravens, the trade was called off and Baltimore signed Trey Hendrickson.

title: Women's Bracketology: Champ Week upsets ripple through the field
content: Dreams are being realized and crushed during Champ Week. Stephen F. Austin pulled the latest upset to clinch an auto-bid.

title: Women's college basketball awards and All-Americans: UConn's Sarah Strong leads way
content: Mikayla Blakes made sure POY wasn't a one-player race, and Aaliyah Chavez had company in the FOY talk.

title: Ranking the best remaining free agents: Top unsigned players
content: Where could Aaron Rodgers and Deebo Samuel land? Let's stack the top free agents who are still available.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: Prediction markets' unpredictable future amid conflicting court rulings
content: Regulatory authority is at the center of growing battle in courts, Congress, statehouses and the CFTC.

title: Caminero's dominant WBC debut -- and what it means for his Rays future
content: The young star is thriving on the international stage, setting himself up for a strong 2026 campaign.

title: Can the Knicks get Towns, Brunson on track? Five issues they need to solve -- fast
content: The Knicks are 42-25 and third in the Eastern Conference, but they have a few problems to solve if they want to make a deep playoff run.

title: NBA Power Rankings: Thunder, Spurs rule the West, Celtics rise
content: The NBA playoffs are near. Which teams are best positioned for the homestretch of the regular season?

title: Tension builds ahead of NFLPA leadership vote
content: NFL Players Association representatives are set to vote on a successor to disgraced former leader Lloyd Howell.

title: Why Miami is facing its 'most challenging' offseason yet of the Cristobal era
content: Can Miami reach college football's mountaintop again? Mario Cristobal has the Canes ready to climb again.

title: Premier League, LaLiga ... and Scotland? Most exciting races in Europe, 2025-26
content: Don't look now, but Scotland's top league might be won by a club that hasn't done it since 1960! Bill Connelly breaks down the most exciting races at the top and bottom of Europe's many, many leagues.

title: Olympic champ Alysa Liu receives key to city in hometown rally
content: The Olympic figure skating champion received a key to her hometown during a rally that included performances from Bay Area artists.

title: Three takeaways from Team USA's win, Caitlin Clark's debut
content: Six players, including Clark, made their debuts for the U.S. national team. How did they look in USA's win?

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: Talks in 'right direction' as marathon meeting continues
content: League personnel and WNBPA staff have met for three consecutive days but have not yet reached a new collective bargaining agreement.

title: Betting on the Oscars: From 'Marty Supreme' to 'KPop Demon Hunters'
content: Do you think you know who's winning at the Oscars? See what the sportsbooks say, then make your final picks.

title: Ravens tied for second-shortest Super Bowl odds after whirlwind week
content: Every NFL team's team's win total, as well as odds to win their division, the conference and Super Bowl LXI.

title: Empathy is complicated in a selfish, results-based business like NASCAR
content: As soon as the green flag waves, drivers are all out for themselves, but are there instances when the stars of NASCAR feel empathy for their rivals?

title: NWSL 2026 mega-preview: Key moves, questions and predictions for all 16 teams
content: The NWSL is back! Let's predict how each team will finish by assessing what's ahead for Rodman, Lavelle and the other stars.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal eye move for Leipzig's Lukeba
content: Arsenal are considering a move for RB Leipzig defender Castello Lukeba. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: Stronger air defence takeaway for India from West Asia conflict: Ex IAF chief
content: “Drones are going to play a “huge role” in any forthcoming future conflict that we can envisage,” the former IAF chief said

title: Six MPs urge Centre to include reservation Act of Karnataka in Ninth Schedule
content: In the memorandum dated March 10, the MPs expressed concern that the proposal sent by the government of Karnataka to include the Act in the Ninth Schedule has been pending with the Union Government for more than three years

title: GMIT to hold 21st convocation in Davanagere on March 14
content: A total of 708 students including 649 graduates (B.E.) students and 59 postgraduates (MBA) students will receive their degrees during the 21st convocation of GM Institute of Technology (GMIT) in Davanagere on March 14

title: On the streets for survival
content: Economic hardship, social customs, displacement, and sudden life setbacks continue to push people into begging even as rehabilitation efforts struggle to keep pace. Nellore Sravani reports from Vijayawada on the lives caught between survival and law

title: NITI Aayog praises Kalaburagi district in Karnataka for achieving 100% institutional deliveries
content: Earlier in November 2025, Kalaburagi district’s administration had been appreciated by NITI Aayog for implementing innovative governance and development initiatives to ensure sustained progress in its aspirational blocks

title: All crest gates of Tungabhadra dam to be replaced by May: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar
content: As of March 10, the installation of 14 crest gates had been completed. Work on three more gates was in progress. According to technical experts, installation of one crest gate normally takes about seven days.

title: Neighbourhood clinics on crutches
content: Across Hyderabad’s crowded localities, Urban Primary Health Centres serve as the first point of contact for thousands seeking everyday medical care. Often housed in ageing buildings and run by small teams of doctors, nurses and community health workers, these clinics shoulder much of the city’s routine healthcare burden. But while the city gains prominence for its corporate hospitals and medical tourism, UPHCs continue to function with limited infrastructure and growing patient loads, writes Siddharth Kumar Singh

title: Kalaburagi-Bengaluru Vande Bharat Express train completes two years of service, records occupancy rate of 103.52% in 2026
content: The train covers a distance of 561 km in about eight hours, compared with 10 to 12 hours by other superfast trains, with halts at Yadgir, Raichur, Manthralayam Road, Guntakal, Anantapur, Sai P. Nilayam and Yelahanka

title: Former West Bengal Governor Ananda Bose buries hatchet with Educationists’ Forum head Omprakash Mishra
content: Professor Omprakash Mishra called former Governor an erudite person and said that he was polite and generous with his appreciation during the meeting

title: Rajisha Vijayan: I was flabbergasted when Krishand first told me the story of ‘Masthishka Maranam’
content: Rajisha Vijayan talks about how she transformed to become Frida Soman in the sci-fi satire Masthishka Maranam - A Frankenbiting of Simon’s Memories, directed by Krishand

title: LPG supply fears spark induction stove rush in India
content: West Asia tensions disrupt LPG supply chains, pushing households and restaurants toward electric cooking alternatives

title: Four killed as bus overturns on NH-44 in Nizamabad district
content: The deceased were identified as Manjusha, 30, her seven-year-old son Rishi. While the body of Abbas, 40, has been identified, the identity of another male victim is yet to be established

title: The Supreme Court judgement on creamy layer exclusion for OBC quota | Explained
content: The judgement came on March 11 in a case that was meant to clear decades-long confusion about how to apply the crucial income/wealth test prescribed in regulations, specifically for OBC candidates whose parents are officials of Central or State PSUs or in private employment

title: Experts clash over HALEU-Th fuel for Indian nuclear reactors
content: Indian scientists and international experts are divided over a study questioning the viability of a nuclear reactor fuel called HALEU-thorium; while critics cite safety risks, proponents argue the technology is vital to achieve national energy goals

title: 29 new bridges proposed on Musi river in Hyderabad
content: The Comprehensive Mobility Plan under preparation as precursor to the master plan of Hyderabad Metropolitan Region for 2050, has proposed 29 bridges on the Musi river

title: India-made app turns impaired speech into clear speech in near-realtime
content: The delay is only a few hundred milliseconds, and the developers are now focusing on regional languages, project leader Vineet Gandhi says

title: CII-Rajasthan annual session held with call to promote entrepreneurship among youth
content: The Chief Minister said the State government had introduced as many as 34 new policies to create a conducive environment for industries

title: Cost escalation and delays haunt Bengaluru Metro project
content: Bengaluru has India’s second largest metro network, with around 96 kilometres of operational track and 83 stations. Darshan Devaiah B.P. argues that this key transport project, built with ₹67,460 crore so far, needs to keep deadlines to be cost effective and serve the public better

title: Rumours being spread about LPG shortage, says Punjab CM Mann
content: Cautioning against attempts to exploit the situation, the Chief Minister said the government will act firmly against anyone trying to create panic or manipulate the supply of essential goods

title: WB BJP leaders meet PM ahead of mega Brigade Rally in Kolkata
content: The meeting comes 48 hours before the Prime Minister is scheduled to address a mega rally at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday. According to sources in the party, the first list of BJP candidates contesting Assembly polls is likely to be announced in the next few days

title: Akhilesh Yadav meets Avimukteshwaranand Saraswati, vows to put an end to ‘fake saints’
content: Samajwadi Party chief said the responsibility of the LPG crisis in India is on the BJP government, both at the Centre and State in U.P.

title: Goa, desired and disputed: Review of Appetite, an anthology of stories, essays and poems
content: The collection attempts to look beyond clichés, to provoke debates around belonging, settlement and identity

title: In Bihar, companies surrendering sand mining lease will not be allowed to participate in fresh tender
content: 78 companies surrendered sand mining leases citing massive losses. The Bihar minister said the move has caused a revenue loss to the tune of around ₹700 crore to the department

title: VBSA Bill a ‘solution’ to current challenges, ‘catalyst’ for growth: UGC tells joint panel of Parliament
content: Education Ministry had told the joint panel that the NEP 2020 was prepared after one of the widest consultation processes; the Bill was circulated amongst 39 Central Ministries and Departments for consultations

title: CSR Initiative to provide mental health education to 10,000 students in Karnataka and Tamil Nadu
content: These activity-based sessions are designed to make conversations around emotions, empathy, communication, and well-being accessible to young people and educators alike

title: Missiles and drones strike at the heart of a village in Odisha
content: The war in West Asia has thrown the world into chaos. Its impact is even being felt in a remote village in Odisha, where almost every family has a male member working in the Gulf. Satyasundar Barik speaks to residents of Manikapur in Ganjam district about how they are coping with the anxiety

title: Kochi Corporation weighs fresh options as KSINC faces criticism over ro-ro service
content: Move comes after councillors across party lines voiced sharp criticism of KSINC over mounting losses and urged the civic body to identify a new operator to ease its financial burden

title: LPG crisis: PM Modi hits out at those creating panic; asks States to monitor black marketers, hoarders
content: The prime minister also said the government is actively working on multiple fronts, and in the past few days, he spoke with the leaders of several countries about the developments in West Asia

title: Kerala nurses on the warpath over long-overdue wage revision
content: Hundreds of nurses working in private hospitals across Kerala are on an indefinite strike in pursuit of their demands, including a wage revision. Aathira Haridas listens to the grievances of the protesters

title: HC disqualification of SNDP Yogam director board a test of Kerala govt.’s mettle
content: Given Vellappally Natesan’s close ties with the ruling establishment, the Kerala government will have a tough task abiding by the High Court’s directive to temporarily take charge of the Yogam and to set up a provisional board to oversee affairs till elections are held

title: Behind the crumbling walls of Bihar’s Balirajgarh Fort, a tale of monumental neglect
content: The approval, issued by the ASI on February 26, is valid for a year and authorises the agency’s Patna Circle to undertake excavation at the site, officially listed as the ‘Remains of Ancient Fort of Garh’

title: Kerala Assembly polls: LDF’s development narrative in Kollam constituency will face a stiff challenge from UDF
content: While the constituency has traditionally leaned towards the left, the Congress sprang a major surprise by winning Kollam Corporation in the recent local body elections. The BJP too has steadily raised its vote share

title: PM Modi speaks to Iranian President, discusses 'serious' situation in West Asia
content: The Prime Minister told Masoud Pezeshkian that the safety and security of Indian nationals, along with the need for unhindered transit of goods and energy, remain India's top priorities

title: Lacking long-term storage, India must rely on imports to manage LPG supply
content: In 2024-25, India’s LPG production was some 13 million tonnes as per government data; LPG consumption was around 31 million tonnes that year. Broadly, there is a 60-40 import-export split

title: India recieved requests for energy supplies from Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and Maldives, says MEA
content: India’s refining capacity, our own requirements, and diesel availability will be factored in while taking decisions, said MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal

title: Transpersons, men having sex with men, sex workers cannot donate blood, Centre insists in Supreme Court
content: Public health perspective must trump individual rights, says Ministry, noting that the right of the recipient to receive a safe blood transfusion far outweighs the right of an individual to donate blood

title: Inter departmental panel likely to check contamination in vegetables
content: Forest and Ecology Minister Eshwar Khandre says a meeting of Bengaluru legislators to be convened to discuss the issue shortly

title: Congress urges government to hold an all‑party meeting on women’s quota law
content: The government had reached out to at least two parties — the Congress and the Samajwadi Party — seeking their opinion on advancing the timeline for implementing the legislation

title: Railways launch drive against liquor sale in trains by aboard  staff
content: Action comes amid reports that housekeeping staff, pantry/catering and vendors were involved in illegal provision or sale of liquor, gutkha among other prohibited items on running trains

title: "Undefeated": White House Shares Wii Sports-Themed Iran Strike Video
content: The video shows game characters hitting targets before cutting to footage of American strikes on Iran.

title: Gold, Silver Prices Rise Amid War Uncertainity: Check Rates In Your City
content: Gold, Silver Prices On March 13: The price of 24-carat gold slipped Rs 10 in early trade, with 10 grams of the metal priced at Rs 1,62,210 in India.

title: Adobe's Shantanu Narayen To Step Down As CEO After 18 Years
content: Adobe said that its long-serving chief executive Shantanu Narayen, will step down after nearly two decades in the role, triggering a leadership search at one of the world's most influential software companies.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates: Markets Open Lower Again, Rupee Hits Fresh Low Against Dollar
content: Stock Market Live Updates Sensex Today: Indian benchmark indices saw another low opening on Friday as crude oil prices again crossed the $100-mark after a brief respite.

title: Dubai Hit Again: Video Shows Smoke Above Buildings, Burj Khalifa In Background
content: Buildings in Dubai shook on Friday after loud explosions were heard, and a thick cloud of smoke rose over a central part of the city.

title: US Warship Abraham Lincoln Hit By Iran Strike? Claim And Counter-Claim
content: Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy has claimed that its ballistic missiles have struck the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln, rendering it non-operational and forcing it to retreat from the Gulf waters.

title: US-Israel Iran War LIVE Updates: Indian Markets Set to Open Lower, US Allows Russian Oil Purchase
content: US-Israel Iran Attacks LIVE Updates: India's stock markets are likely to open lower on Friday, posing a possibility of the worst drop since December 2024 as the benchmark reports a plunge amid the Middle East conflict.

title: Video: Disguised As Doctor, Hyderabad Woman Enters Hospital, Steals Patient's Gold
content: A woman entered a reputed private hospital in Hyderabad dressed as doctor and stole gold from a patient.

title: "Ring Roads A Big Flop": Supreme Court Hikes Entry Fee For Trucks In Delhi
content: Heavy and light commercial vehicles, including trucks, vans and dumpers, entering Delhi will have to pay higher Environment Compensation Charges (ECC) from April 1.

title: "Wouldn't Issue Life Insurance": Netanyahu Threatens Mojtaba Khemenei
content: Netanayahu said that Iran was "no longer the same" after nearly two weeks of bombardment and that Tehran had suffered blows to its elite Revolutionary Guards Corps and Basij paramilitary force.

title: Iran-Backed Group Claims It Downed American Jet, Contradicts US Version
content: Iran's state media has said that Iraq's resistance groups shot down a US military aircraft in western Iraq, claiming it was hit by a missile. But the United States has firmly denied this, saying the aircraft was not brought down by hostile fire.

title: Trump Says Mojtaba Khamenei Alive 'In Some Form' Amid War Injury Reports
content: US President Donald Trump believes Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader, is still alive "in some form," despite not being seen publicly since taking office after the war began in Iran.

title: Rupee Nears 93, Inflation At 10-Month High: What It Means For Your Money
content: Iran War Impact: The conflict in the Middle East has disrupted energy markets and pushed crude oil prices above $100 a barrel, raising concerns about both inflation and currency stability.

title: Trump Claims US Will Profit From Rising Oil Prices, Iran's Nuke Reply
content: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has claimed that during talks mediated by Oman with the United States, Tehran had proposed to seize any plan to develop nuclear weapons.

title: After India, US Temporarily Allows Sale Of Russian Oil Already At Sea
content: The United States is temporarily allowing the sale of Russian oil that is at sea, the Treasury Department said Thursday, as energy prices soared after US-Israeli strikes on Iran plunged the Middle East into war.

title: Footfall Drop, Raw Materials Delayed: Ajmal Perfumes Chief On War Impact
content: NDTV visited the manufacturing facility of Ajmal Perfumes in Dubai, one of the region's largest perfume brands, to understand how the situation is affecting the market.

title: Netanyahu Says Top Iranian Nuclear Scientist Killed In Israeli Strikes
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says Israeli attacks have killed top Iranian nuclear scientists.

title: Hundreds Homeless As Massive Fire Burns Down 80 Delhi Shanties
content: On the night on March 11, a ferocious fire tore through nearly four acres of the bustling Machhli Mandi slum cluster in Matiala village near Delhi's Uttam Nagar, reducing homes to smouldering ash and charred ruins within hours.

title: US Military Aircraft Crashes In Iraq, 4th American Plane Lost During Iran War
content: An American KC-135 aerial refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq while a second plane involved in the incident landed safely, the US military said Thursday.

title: Reports of Iran allowing Indian ships through Strait of Hormuz ‘premature’: Centre
content: India has been expressing concern about the targeting of merchant navy ships that are stuck both inside the Gulf as well as in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz

title: Beer sales drop by 14.59% in Karnataka; govt. cites Andhra excise policy, weather
content: As per the latest figures, the State had generated ₹37,531 crore in excise revenue, which is about ₹4,000 crore short of the target set for the current financial year

title: Kochi Corporation to fast-track Konthuruthy compensation
content: Corporation secretary to initiate steps to distribute compensation to families residing on Konthuruthy river puramboke under the special rehabilitation package approved by the State Cabinet

title: No shortage of fuel in country, says government
content: There is no shortage of fuel and crude supply is secured, foremost priority of govt. is to provide cooking gas to households, regulation of commercial LPG will prevent black-marketing, says Petroleum Minister

title: Defence Forces Vision 2047 calls for space, cyber command and drone force
content: The strategic road map document, accessed by The Hindu, outlines broad goals that the armed forces must aspire to achieve as India prepares its military for evolving security challenges and the centenary of Independence

title: Supreme Court to look into what constitutes ‘personal data’ in DPDP laws
content: The DPDP laws in effect legalise disproportionate state surveillance, create a compensation vacuum for citizens, dilute the Right to Information, erode the ability of journalists to practice their profession, the petition said

title: India co-sponsors resolution passed by UNSC condemning Iran attacks on Gulf
content: India prioritises safety of ‘all civilians’ says MEA, but doesn’t criticise U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran and Lebanon

title: Parental income alone cannot decide OBC creamy layer status: Supreme Court
content: Rules in favour of children of senior PSU officials selected as OBC candidates in civil service exams; creamy layer exclusion framework says income from salaries, farm land cannot be included for wealth test

title: LPG shortage forces eateries to shut down, disrupts wedding catering in Bengaluru
content: The crisis has also begun to affect the city’s wedding catering industry, with several caterers halting new bookings and scaling down elaborate wedding menus

title: Supreme Court allows withdrawal of plea to bring back Netaji's mortal remains from Tokyo temple
content: The court said it would prefer to have Netaji’s sole heir and daughter, Anita Bose Pfaff, approach it with the prayer directly

title: LPG shortage: Kerala CM urges distributors to prioritise commercial gas supply for essential services
content: An urgent meeting chaired by Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan directed suppliers to give greater priority to hospitals, old-age homes, orphanages, schools, community kitchens, hostels, IT parks and factory canteens

title: No shortage of piped natural gas in Bengaluru: GAIL Gas
content: GAIL Gas spokesperson says hotels and eateries that are using commercial LPG currently and are concerned about supply can switch to PNG line subject to the availability within a matter of two months

title: West Asia conflict: Saudi oil tanker reaches Mumbai after crossing Strait of Hormuz
content: Shenlong, a tanker managed by Dynacom, sailed from Ras Tanura in Saudi Arabia and docked at the Mumbai port at 4.30 p.m. on March 11, according to a ship-tracking website

title: Madras High Court stays suit filed by PMK founder Ramadoss against his estranged son Anbumani
content: Justice T.V. Thamilselvi grants the interim stay till April 10, 2026 pursuant to a civil revision petition filed by party general secretary Vadivel Ravanan

title: Mamata plans protest against LPG, CNG crisis on March 16
content: Trinamool leadership criticises the Central government for its foreign policy decisions resulting in the rising fuel crisis

title: ISRO, AIIMS sign MoU for cooperation in space medicine and research
content: The memorandum aims to promote joint research aligned with ISRO’s priority areas, with a shared objective of advancing human health, performance and safety during human space missions, says the space agency

title: Nitish-led NDA government of 21 years failed in all parameters in Bihar, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: BJP leader Niraj Kumar hits back at the RJD leader, says NDA’s double-engine government in Bihar had pulled the State out of deterioration since 2005 and firmly placed it on the path to development

title: President’s secretariat declines TMC plea for appointment citing time constraints
content: The move comes days after President Droupadi Murmu expressed displeasure over arrangements during her West Bengal visit, triggering a political row

title: LPG shortage hits railway station kitchens; onboard catering continues
content: Hotel owners at the busy Krantivira Sangolli Rayanna Railway Station say the shortage has forced them to alter their menus and limit items that require continuous gas usage

title: LPG situation under control, no need for panic booking, says A.P. govt.
content: LPG stock of 17,962 MT available, steps being taken to ensure uninterrupted fuel supplies and protect households and priority sectors, announces govt.

title: Kharge blames Centre for ‘security collapse’ after attack on Farooq Abdullah
content: Congress president alleges plot to target senior leaders, says law and order has broken down since J&K lost Statehood; Nadda calls it a “murderous attack” but accuses Opposition of politicising the issue

title: ‘No, don’t do that’: Hillary Clinton pulls back as Bill nudges her toward busy crosswalk

title: The ACC's most important newcomers and what their teams are saying
